# R3 · Reliable Queues — Redis Patterns Applied

> The heart of the EchoMQ grounding: a job is never lost — moved into an in-flight list under a lock, delivered at
> least once to an idempotent consumer, reclaimed if its worker dies, transitioned atomically, and picked up without
> busy-polling. Grounded in EchoMQ's real worker fetch loop (the iconic Elixir app echo/apps/echomq). The pivot
> chapter; it surveys the course direction through R8 and opens the door into the living EchoMQ course. Served at
> /redis-patterns/queues. The chapter is fully built: three orientation dives plus the six granular modules
> R3.01–R3.06.

## The chapter — three deep dives (built)

- [R3 · The reliable queue](/redis-patterns/queues/the-reliable-queue): the reliable-queue family in one place — RPOPLPUSH wait→active under a lock (moveToActive-11.lua), at-least-once + de:{id} idempotency (EchoMQ.Keys.dedup/2), stalled reclaim (moveStalledJobsToWait-8.lua).
- [R3 · States as locations](/redis-patterns/queues/states-as-locations): the lifecycle as one atomic Lua move — states are Redis locations, moveToFinished-14.lua is one EVALSHA (EchoMQ.Scripts.execute_raw/4), and emq:{queue}:marker + BZPOPMIN replaces busy-polling.
- [R3 · The road ahead](/redis-patterns/queues/the-road-ahead): where the queue goes next — the arc R3→R8 and the door into the living EchoMQ course (R3 → E2/E5/E6, per echomq.toc.md).

## The granular modules — each a hub + three dives (the workshop is a capstone, no dives)

- [R3.01 · Processing list](/redis-patterns/queues/processing-list): move a job into an in-flight list so a crash is recoverable — the wait/active LISTs, the atomic RPOPLPUSH/LMOVE transfer (moveToActive-11.lua), the in-flight list as the recovery ledger. Dives: list-wait-active · lmove-rpoplpush · the-in-flight-list. (built)
- [R3.02 · At-least-once](/redis-patterns/queues/at-least-once): delivery guarantees and why consumers must be idempotent — at-least-once semantics, idempotent consumers, why exactly-once is a lie; grounded in producer-side de:{id} dedup (EchoMQ.Keys.dedup/2). Dives: at-least-once-semantics · idempotent-consumers · why-exactly-once-is-a-lie. (built)
- [R3.03 · Stalled recovery](/redis-patterns/queues/stalled-recovery): reclaim jobs whose worker died — lock-expiry detection, two-phase mark/recover, the atomic moveStalledJobsToWait-8.lua sweep vs the non-atomic Go loop. Dives: lock-expiry-detection · two-phase-mark-recover · atomic-vs-non-atomic. (built)
- [R3.04 · Atomic state machine](/redis-patterns/queues/atomic-state-machine): the whole lifecycle as one atomic Lua move — states are Redis locations, moveToFinished-14.lua transitions a job (drop the lock, record the result, fetch the next) in one EVALSHA (EchoMQ.Scripts.move_to_finished/7), with the EVALSHA→NOSCRIPT→EVAL fallback. Dives: states-as-locations · read-decide-write-in-one-evalsha · evalsha-and-noscript. (built)
- [R3.05 · Blocking vs polling](/redis-patterns/queues/blocking-vs-polling): stop busy-polling the queue — the busy-poll cost, the blocking pop, the marker wake-up; a worker blocks on emq:{queue}:marker with BZPOPMIN (EchoMQ.Worker.wait_for_job/2) and wakes when a job add runs ZADD marker, vs the Go time.Sleep poll. Dives: the-busy-poll-cost · blocking-pop · the-marker-wake-up. (built)
- [R3.06 · Workshop](/redis-patterns/queues/workshop): the capstone — a reliable Portal enrollment-job queue assembling R3.01–R3.05 (wait → active under a lock → finish, with stalled recovery and the marker). At-least-once delivery plus Portal's already-idempotent enroll (Portal.Enrollment.enroll/2, the :already_enrolled guard) is exactly-once-in-effect. Applied design — Portal does not run on EchoMQ today. No dives. (built)

## The door to EchoMQ

R3 opens onto the living EchoMQ course (a depth course that tracks the EMQ build rung by rung): E2 · The lifecycle, components & runtimes (/echomq/core) · E5 · Batches (/echomq/batches) · E6 · Lifecycle controls (/echomq/lifecycle). Served as the EchoMQ build lands.

## Up

- [Course home](/redis-patterns) · [R2 · Coordination](/redis-patterns/coordination) · next: R4 · Time, Delay & Priority (soon)
